description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
A Ghost ADS-equipped car was driving engaged Northbound on Highway 17. As always, there were two safety drivers in the car. Traffic 
was moving in all four lanes at or near the speed limit. The Ghost vehicle was in the right-middle lane. Another vehicle (the "lead" car) 
entered into the Ghost car's lane from the right. While there were numerous vehicles ahead of the Ghost car before that vehicle entered the 
lane, the lead vehicle blocked the view of the lane before it. Throughout the incident, traffic in the other three lanes continued at a steady 
pace. When the lead vehicle entered into the lane ahead of the Ghost car, the Ghost car applied the brakes to accommodate the intrusion and 
then continued to match the speed of the lead car. The lead car suddenly slammed the brakes. The Ghost car followed suit, reducing its 
speed from 19 mps (42.5 mph) to 4.7 mps (10.5 mph) within the next three seconds. The Ghost car was able to match the rapid speed 
reduction of the lead car and avoid contact. The driver following the Ghost car was not able to respond as quickly as the Ghost ADS. When 
the following driver began to apply the brakes, the car was about five meters behind and was traveling at 6.5 mps. At the point of impact, 
the Ghost vehicle was traveling at 4.78 mps (10.7 mph), matching the lead car. There were no injuries. The vehicles suffered only cosmetic 
damage, the drivers exchanged information, and the Ghost safety drivers returned to home base in conventional mode.
Print
Clear Form
